task_completion.visual_evidence: The DreamPlan Home Design Software application window is open and visible on the screen.
The 'Sample House' project is loaded in the workspace, identifiable by the layout or the project name in the title bar or recent files list.

task_completion.description: Delete the interior wall separating the Kitchen from the Dining/Living area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D floor plan or 3D view, there is a clear, unobstructed open space between the Kitchen area (identifiable by appliances/counters) and the Dining/Living area. The dividing wall is no longer present.

task_completion.description: Ensure all exterior walls remain intact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outer perimeter walls of the house are fully connected without any missing segments or unintended gaps.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Documents/Projects folder to save the file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box is open, and the current directory path shown in the address bar is 'Documents/Projects' (or a similar OS-specific path ending in Documents/Projects).
The filename field in the save dialog contains 'OpenConcept' (or 'OpenConcept.ddp'), OR the application title bar updates to show 'OpenConcept' as the active project.

integrity.description: The agent used the DreamPlan GUI to delete the wall and save the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on walls, using the delete tool/key, and interacting with the 'File > Save As' menu rather than using a terminal or script to modify the file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual DreamPlan Home Design Software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches DreamPlan Home Design Software (e.g., NCH Software branding, specific toolbars for building/walls), not a generic image editor or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing commands like 'mv', 'cp', or script executions that directly manipulate the project files without GUI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'OpenConcept'. The verified file extension for DreamPlan projects is '.ddp'.
